questions.q: Who killed Indrajit in the war at Lanka?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, Rama's younger brother, slew the sorcerer-prince Indrajit in single combat.

questions.q: Indrajit was the son of which king?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was the eldest son of Ravana, the king of Lanka.

questions.q: What was Indrajit's original birth name?
questions.explanation: He was born Meghanada, named for the thunder-like cry he made at birth.

questions.q: On which side did Indrajit fight in the war?
questions.explanation: Indrajit fought for Lanka, defending his father Ravana against Rama's army.

questions.q: Indrajit was famous for fighting using what kind of warfare?
questions.explanation: Indrajit was a master of illusory warfare, using magic and sorcery to deceive his foes.

questions.q: From whom did Indrajit earn the title 'Indrajit'?
options: By defeating Indra, king of the gods
questions.explanation: He earned the name Indrajit, meaning 'conqueror of Indra,' after defeating Indra, the king of the gods.

questions.q: In which city did the fall of Indrajit take place?
questions.explanation: Indrajit fell during the great war at Lanka, the island kingdom of Ravana.

questions.q: Which weapon did Indrajit most famously use against Rama and Lakshmana early in the war?
questions.explanation: Indrajit bound Rama and Lakshmana with the Nagapasha, the serpent-arrows that ensnared them.

questions.q: Indrajit's death is described as breaking the back of whose resistance?
questions.explanation: The slaying of Indrajit broke the back of Lanka's resistance against Rama's army.

questions.q: Rama and Lakshmana were brothers and sons of which king?
questions.explanation: Rama and Lakshmana were sons of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Where did Indrajit perform his secret yajna (sacrifice) to gain invincible weapons?
questions.explanation: Indrajit performed his sacrifices at the Nikumbhila shrine to become invincible in battle.

questions.q: Who advised Rama that Indrajit must be killed before completing his sacrifice at Nikumbhila?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother who joined Rama, revealed the secret of the Nikumbhila sacrifice.

questions.q: What illusion did Indrajit create to demoralize the vanara army?
options: A false image of Rama's death
A phantom Sita whom he appeared to slay
questions.explanation: Indrajit conjured an illusory Sita and beheaded her before the vanaras to crush their morale.

questions.q: Which vanara leader was deeply grieved by the illusory slaying of Sita and reported it to Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman witnessed the illusory killing of Sita and, grief-stricken, carried the news to Rama.

questions.q: Who accompanied Lakshmana to guide him to Indrajit's secret sacrificial ground?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana guided Lakshmana to the Nikumbhila grove to confront Indrajit before his rite was complete.

questions.q: Earlier in the war, who released Rama and Lakshmana from Indrajit's serpent-bonds?
questions.explanation: Garuda, the divine eagle and enemy of serpents, arrived and freed Rama and Lakshmana from the Nagapasha.

questions.q: On an earlier occasion, with which divine weapon did Indrajit strike down both Rama and Lakshmana, leaving the army despairing?
questions.explanation: Indrajit bound Rama and Lakshmana with the Naga-pasha, his serpent-arrows; they were freed when Garuda, the foe of serpents, appeared and the snakes fled.

questions.q: What made Indrajit nearly invincible while fighting from his chariot in the sky?
questions.explanation: Indrajit could turn invisible, attacking unseen from the sky, which made him extremely difficult to fight.

questions.q: What strategic reason made interrupting Indrajit's Nikumbhila rite essential?
options: Completing it would make him truly invincible
It would burn the vanara bridge
It would bring Kumbhakarna back to life
questions.explanation: If Indrajit completed the sacrifice, he would emerge invincible, so he had to be slain before it finished.

questions.q: Which of these warriors did NOT directly help corner Indrajit at the sacrificial grove?
questions.explanation: Ravana remained in Lanka; it was Lakshmana, Vibhishana, and the vanaras who attacked Indrajit's grove.

questions.q: After his sacrifice was disrupted, how did Indrajit and Lakshmana finally settle the contest?
options: A fierce duel of arrows and divine weapons
questions.explanation: Forced from his rite, Indrajit fought Lakshmana in a prolonged duel of arrows and astras.

questions.q: Which divine weapon did Lakshmana invoke to deliver the fatal blow to Indrajit?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana invoked the Aindrastra, the weapon of Indra, beheading Indrajit in their final duel.

questions.q: By tradition, a key reason Lakshmana could slay Indrajit was that Lakshmana had observed what?
options: A vow of silence for twelve years
Celibacy and not sleeping for fourteen years in exile
A pilgrimage to all sacred rivers
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that a boon required Indrajit be slain by one who had conquered sleep and was celibate for fourteen years, which Lakshmana had done in exile.

questions.q: What was the name of Indrajit's chief wife, whose grief is noted in some retellings of his death?
questions.explanation: Sulochana, daughter of the serpent king, is named in later traditions as Indrajit's grieving wife.

questions.q: Whom did Indrajit defeat to earn his celebrated title, and what was given to ensure his safe return from that conquest?
options: He defeated Indra and Brahma granted him a boon
He defeated Yama and gained immortality
He defeated Kubera and won Lanka
He defeated Varuna and won the oceans
questions.explanation: After defeating Indra, Brahma intervened, naming him Indrajit and granting him a protective boon in exchange for releasing Indra.

questions.q: Before the final battle, how many days of combat had Indrajit already waged against Rama's forces in the broader siege of Lanka?
options: He fought across several distinct engagements over multiple days
He never fought before the final day
questions.explanation: Indrajit clashed with Rama's army in several separate engagements during the siege before his final fall.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, which mount or vehicle did Indrajit favor in the final battle against Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Indrajit fought from a chariot, often striking from the sky and using invisibility during the duel.

questions.q: What did the destruction of Indrajit's Nikumbhila sacrifice deny him?
options: A new celestial chariot and invincible weapons
An army of a thousand demons
The ability to revive the dead
questions.explanation: The interrupted rite was meant to grant Indrajit a celestial chariot and invincibility, which he was thereby denied.

questions.q: How did Ravana react upon learning of Indrajit's death?
options: He rejoiced and offered a sacrifice
He was overcome with grief and rage, vowing vengeance
questions.explanation: Ravana was consumed by grief and fury at his son's death and resolved to take revenge himself, even threatening Sita.

questions.q: What was the broader military consequence of Indrajit's fall for the war?
options: It forced Rama to retreat across the sea
It removed Lanka's most powerful warrior, hastening Ravana's own defeat
It ended the war immediately in a truce
questions.explanation: With Indrajit gone, Lanka lost its mightiest defender, setting the stage for Ravana's eventual defeat by Rama.
